core_mechanism: a disorder-driven continuous (second-order) phase transition between localized and extended eigenmodes of a random operator, controlled by a scale-invariant critical point whose critical modes are multifractal and whose critical behavior is organized into universality classes by the operator's symmetries
brief_reason: The abstract frames the phenomenon as transitions between localized and metallic phases in disordered systems, with emphasis on multifractality of critical wave functions and a symmetry classification of the disordered systems governing the criticality, i.e. a symmetry-classified critical point with multifractal critical modes.
